perturbation.original_text: For purposes of clarity, Development shall include any submissions and activities required in support thereof, required by Applicable Laws or a Regulatory Authority as a condition or in support of obtaining a pricing or reimbursement approval for an approved Licensed Product.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of clarity, Exploitation shall include any submissions and activities required in support thereof, required by Applicable Laws or a Regulatory Authority as a condition or in support of obtaining a pricing or reimbursement approval for an approved Licensed Product.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation changes 'Development' to 'Exploitation' in the context of submissions for pricing/reimbursement approval. This creates a contradiction because 'Development' is explicitly defined as activities related to pre-clinical and clinical testing and obtaining regulatory approvals, while 'Exploitation' covers making, using, selling, and offering for sale. This change creates in-text contradiction in section 4.2 where it references diligance to achieve development and obtain Regulatory Approval but with the change made, the development activities, are no longer in that diligence, which then means there is not set diligance as to making sure development activities are taking place, but rather exploitation. This means if development does not take place but there is commercialization, diligance will be met
perturbation.contradicted_text: Following the License Option Exercise Closing Date, AbbVie shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to Commercialize [***] Licensed Product in [***] Major Market following receipt of Regulatory Approval therefor in such Major Market; provided that [***]; provided further that, for purposes of clarity, [***]. [***] If at any time Harpoon has a reasonable basis to believe that AbbVie is in material breach of its obligations under this Section 4.2, then Harpoon may so notify AbbVie, specifying the basis for its belief, and the Parties shall meet within [***] after such notice to discuss in good faith Harpoon's concerns.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the definition of 'Development' to 'Exploitation', creating a contradiction with Section 4.2. The original definition of 'Development' aligns with the diligence required to achieve development and obtain Regulatory Approval. However, changing it to 'Exploitation' means that the required diligence focuses on commercialization rather than development activities. This contradicts Section 4.2, which expects AbbVie to use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to Commercialize the Licensed Product after receiving Regulatory Approval. The diligence obligation is undermined if 'Exploitation' is performed without proper 'Development', potentially leading to a breach of contract if AbbVie fails to pursue Regulatory Approval.

perturbation.original_text: 1.100 "Licensed Product" means any product, or portion thereof, containing a Licensed Compound, alone or in combination with one (1) or more other active ingredients, in any and all forms, in - 10 - Source: HARPOON THERAPEUTICS, INC., 10-K, 3/12/2020 current and future formulations, dosages forms and strengths, and delivery modes, including any improvements thereto. For clarity, Licensed Products that contain the same Licensed Compound (whether or not with one or more active ingredients (if applicable)), but in a different formulation, dosage form or delivery device, shall be considered the same Licensed Product for the purposes of calculating milestone and royalty payments hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.100 "Licensed Compound" means any product, or portion thereof, containing a Licensed Compound, alone or in combination with one (1) or more other active ingredients, in any and all forms, in - 10 - Source: HARPOON THERAPEUTICS, INC., 10-K, 3/12/2020 current and future formulations, dosages forms and strengths, and delivery modes, including any improvements thereto. For clarity, Licensed Products that contain the same Licensed Compound (whether or not with one or more active ingredients (if applicable)), but in a different formulation, dosage form or delivery device, shall be considered the same Licensed Product for the purposes of calculating milestone and royalty payments hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation modifies the definition of "Licensed Product" and changes it to "Licensed Compound". This introduces ambiguity regarding what constitutes a "Licensed Compound" versus a "Licensed Product." The in-text contradiction occurs in sections that deal with First Commercial Sales Milestones or Sales Based Millestones, as that sections talks about milestones being achieved based on the sales of a "Licensed Product". With the change that has been made, this means that Milestones will have to be achieved based on the sale of a "Licensed Compound", which means any product with such licensed compound is being sold, that milestones has been achieved, which is contradictory and doesn't make sense. Which then creates a ambiguity and uncertainty on when these milestones are achieved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In partial consideration of the rights granted by Harpoon to AbbVie hereunder and subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement, AbbVie shall pay to Harpoon the following non-refundable milestone payments due within [***] after the achievement of each of the following milestones, calculated as follows: 6.3.1 upon [***] Licensed Product, [***]; and 6.3.2 upon the First Commercial Sale for the first Licensed Product to achieve such [***], [***]. Each milestone payment in this Section 6.3 shall be payable only upon the first achievement of such milestone and no amounts shall be due for subsequent or repeated achievements of such milestone, whether for the same or a different Licensed Compound or Licensed Product. The maximum aggregate amount payable by AbbVie pursuant to this Section 6.3 is [***].
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Licensed Product" as "Licensed Compound." The contradicted text discusses milestones triggered by "First Commercial Sale for the first Licensed Product". The modification makes it ambiguous whether milestones are triggered by the sale of a "Licensed Compound" or still require a "Licensed Product," creating an inconsistency and uncertainty in the achievement of milestones, which constitutes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: AbbVie shall have the right to list any AbbVie Patents, Joint Patents, Product-Specific Patents, and, upon the written consent of Harpoon, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld, conditioned or delayed (taking into account, without limitation, the potential impact of such consent on Harpoon's platform technology and/or other products undergoing development or commercialization by Harpoon or its Third Party licensees and covered by such Harpoon Patents), other Harpoon Patents, insofar as they cover the Biosimilar Product as required pursuant to Section 351(l) (3)(A), Section 351(l)(5)(b)(i)(II), or Section 351(l)(7) of the PHSA, to respond to any communications with respect to such lists from the filer of the Biosimilar Application, and to negotiate with the filer of the Biosimilar Application as to whether to utilize a different mechanism for information exchange than that specified in Section 351(l) of the PHSA; and (c) [***] shall have the sole right to identify such Patents or respond to communications under any equivalent or similar listing in any other jurisdiction in the Territory. If required pursuant to Applicable Law, [***] shall prepare such lists and make such responses at [***] Harpoon shall cooperate with AbbVie's reasonable requests in connection therewith, including meeting any submission deadlines, in each case, to the extent required or permitted by Applicable Law. AbbVie shall (A) reasonably consult with [***]
perturbation.changed_text: AbbVie shall have the right to list any AbbVie Patents, Joint Patents, Product-Specific Patents, and, upon the written consent of Harpoon, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld, conditioned or delayed (taking into account, without limitation, the potential impact of such consent on Harpoon's platform technology and/or other products undergoing development or commercialization by Harpoon or its Third Party licensees and covered by such Harpoon Patents), other AbbVie Patents, insofar as they cover the Biosimilar Product as required pursuant to Section 351(l) (3)(A), Section 351(l)(5)(b)(i)(II), or Section 351(l)(7) of the PHSA, to respond to any communications with respect to such lists from the filer of the Biosimilar Application, and to negotiate with the filer of the Biosimilar Application as to whether to utilize a different mechanism for information exchange than that specified in Section 351(l) of the PHSA; and (c) [***] shall have the sole right to identify such Patents or respond to communications under any equivalent or similar listing in any other jurisdiction in the Territory. If required pursuant to Applicable Law, [***] shall prepare such lists and make such responses at [***] Harpoon shall cooperate with AbbVie's reasonable requests in connection therewith, including meeting any submission deadlines, in each case, to the extent required or permitted by Applicable Law. AbbVie shall (A) reasonably consult with [***]
perturbation.explanation: This change affects AbbVie's ability to protect the product against biosimilars. Here the text originally read: "...taking into account, without limitation, the potential impact of such consent on Harpoon's platform technology and/or other products undergoing development or commercialization by Harpoon or its Third Party licensees and covered by such Harpoon Patents), other Harpoon Patents..." which is replaced to "...taking into account, without limitation, the potential impact of such consent on Harpoon's platform technology and/or other products undergoing development or commercialization by Harpoon or its Third Party licensees and covered by such Harpoon Patents), other AbbVie Patents...", this replacement affects in text the section where the conduct of Biosimilar Patent Litigation Including Under the Biologics Price Competition and Innovation Act happens, which then means it's a unclear and a In-Text Contradiction is produced where AbbVie is responsible for initiating and managing any biosimilar litigation relating to Licensed Compounds or Licensed Products worldwide. But due to the change, it's also unclear whether Abbvie is the only one who will be able to conduct all Biosimilar litigation due to the change from Harpoon patents to Abbvie Patents. So this creates a overall ambiguity in a important aspect of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Section 7.3, AbbVie shall be responsible for initiating and managing any biosimilar litigation relating to Licensed Compounds or Licensed Products worldwide. AbbVie shall have the first right to bring an action for infringement of the AbbVie Patents, Joint Patents, Product-Specific Patents and, upon the written consent of Harpoon, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld, conditioned or delayed (taking into account, without limitation, the potential impact of such consent on Harpoon's platform technology and/or other products undergoing development or commercialization by Harpoon or its Third Party licensees and covered by such Harpoon Patents), other Harpoon Patents, including as required under Section 351(l)(6) of the PHSA following the agreement on a list of patents for litigation under Section 351(l)(4) or exchange of Patent lists pursuant to Section 351(l) (5)(B) of such act, or as required following any equivalent or similar certification or notice in any other jurisdiction. If Harpoon decides pursuant to this Agreement not to allow AbbVie to include such other Harpoon Patents in a litigation against a biosimilar applicant for a biosimilar product, Harpoon shall not assert such Patent in any litigation against the same biosimilar applicant for the same biosimilar product without written approval by AbbVie. The Parties' rights and obligations with respect to the foregoing legal actions shall be as set forth in Sections 7.3.1 through 7.3.5; provided that within [***] of reaching agreement on a list of Patents for litigation under Section 351(l)(4) or exchange of Patent lists pursuant to Section 351(l) (5)(B), AbbVie shall notify Harpoon as to whether or not it elects to prosecute such infringement. Either Party shall, within [***], notify and provide the other Party with copies of any notice of commercial marketing provided by the filer of a Biosimilar Application pursuant to Section 351(l)(8)(A) of the PHSA, or any equivalent or similar certification or notice in any other jurisdiction. Thereafter, AbbVie shall have the first right to seek an injunction or other remedies against such commercial marketing as permitted pursuant to Section 351(l)(8)(B) of the PHSA.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Harpoon Patents' to 'AbbVie Patents' in the modified text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text makes it clear that Harpoon retains certain rights and control over its own patents in the context of biosimilar litigation, particularly requiring their consent for AbbVie to include Harpoon patents in litigation. The changed text, however, suggests that AbbVie can unilaterally decide on the use of these patents, undermining Harpoon's rights and creating ambiguity about who controls the biosimilar litigation process, especially concerning Harpoon's patents, triggering Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction. Specifically, AbbVie's responsibility for managing biosimilar litigation involving Licensed Products worldwide (from the Contradicted Text) is now unclear due to the change related to which 'other' patents are under consideration ('Harpoon' vs. 'AbbVie').
